# Transcrição: My_Journey_to_Becoming_More_CONFIDENT

- **Fonte:** My_Journey_to_Becoming_More_CONFIDENT
- **Duração:** 03:01
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Everyone wants to be more confident, but they miss the most important ingredient. When I was young, about 13 years old, I had a friend named Kevin. We're not very close anymore. Kevin and I bonded over the fact that we were both massive losers, and we never dared to speak to girls. But I remember this one moment during school camp. I could hear people clapping, so I followed the sounds to see what people were doing, and it led me to the campfire area. And I couldn't believe my eyes as I approached the campfire. Never have I felt more betrayed in my life. There was Kevin sitting around the fire pit, playing his ukulele, and talking to girls. I felt so angry, I felt again so betrayed. I stormed back to the cabin and pretended to go to sleep. I didn't even talk to him the entire night, even when he walked in and said, Vin? Vin? To me, I was like, whew! I woke up the next morning and again Kevin wasn't there. And I remember thinking to myself, great, he's probably talking to girls again. I walked to the breakfast area, because that's where everyone gathered in the morning. And what I saw again shot the hell out of me. Kevin was sitting by himself, and I thought to myself, what the hell? But then very quickly, I thought to myself, yes, there's my friend, there's the Kevin I know. I got my friend back. This moment always stuck with me, because it confused me. I didn't know what happened, and I never really quite understood it. Let me explain to you what happened. On that night when Kevin was playing the ukulele, he was contextually confident in that moment. Because he was good at the ukulele, when he plays it, it makes him feel confident. The next morning, when he was at the breakfast table, he didn't have his ukulele, which meant that in that context, he wasn't confident. Understanding contextual confidence is crucial for this next point that I'm about to make. The reason why communication skills are so important is because when you learn how to master this instrument, your ability to communicate, think about this for a moment. Can you leave your voice at home? Can you leave your ability to story tell at home? Can you leave your masterful use of body language at home? No. And do you know what this means? This means that as you master your instrument, being your communication ability, you become contextually confident in every single area of your life. This is the biggest key to confidence that most people rarely talk about. Everyone wants to be more confident, but they miss developing a vital skill, communication skills. I've run two free master classes this year, and I'm about to run my final free master class for 2023. If you want to sign up for this master class, please check out the link in the comments. I've got two time zones to try to accommodate as many of you as possible. When you click that link, you'll see two different time zones that are converted into your local time zone to keep things simple. Just pick the one that works best for you. I can't wait to see you there.
